title: Quararibea alchornifolia
intro: Quararibea alchornifolia, also known as Chachacoma, is a medicinal plant native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Quararibea alchornifolia contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, including quararin, quararibin, and alchornin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
taxonomy: Quararibea alchornifolia is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Ochnaceae and the order Malpighiales. This classification places Quararibea alchornifolia within the larger group of flowering plants. Further, it is a part of the clade Angiosperms, which comprises all flowering plants. The genus Quararibea is a distinct group within the Ochnaceae family. Quararibea alchornifolia's classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family and order.
benefits: I couldn't find any information about the specific health benefits of Quararibea alchornifolia.
